summary: V5.4 Admin Configuration + Secondary Email + Tech Offer FV PDF + Polished Hubs. Backend: 15/16 pytest passed, 1 skipped (no documents to send email — expected). Frontend: All critical flows pass — admin/config page renders fully (hero with 6 stat cards 21/3/19/18/7/0, maintenance toggle, banner editor with severity chips, SMTP form, feature flags grid, users table), /settings shows secondary-email-section + admin-config-link, persistence across reload OK, Forum compose modal opens, FV PDF downloads after calculation (oferta-tehnica-FV-10kWp.pdf), Industries hub renders 12 cards with progress bars + status chips + 3 hero stats.

minor.issue: Returns 405 — no GET handler defined; only PATCH /users/me exists. Frontend reads user via /api/auth/me. Spec says GET /api/users/me should return secondary_email — either add the GET handler or update spec/docs to point to /api/auth/me.
Response body is {ok, updates, user_id} — does not echo the full updated user. Caller must re-fetch to confirm state. Consider returning the updated user document for cleaner UX.

integration_issues.issue: Review request mentions route '/calcul-fotovoltaic' but actual route is '/fotovoltaic' (App.js:79 and AppShell.jsx:23). Once on correct route, PDF download works perfectly.

issues: Modal does not use role='dialog' (a11y) — Shadcn Dialog usually adds it; consider verifying it's the shadcn Dialog and not a custom div.

action_items: Decide: either add GET /api/users/me handler returning the current user, OR update the V5.4 spec to use GET /api/auth/me. Frontend already uses /api/auth/me so functional impact is zero, but the spec mismatch is misleading.
Update review-request docs/spec: actual photovoltaic route is /fotovoltaic, NOT /calcul-fotovoltaic.
(Optional) Return the updated user object in PATCH /api/admin/users/{id} response for symmetry with PUT /api/admin/config which already echoes the new state.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to 2060 lines — recommend extracting /admin/* endpoints into a dedicated router module (admin.py) similar to forum.py for maintainability (>700-line guideline).

Developer-implicit-admin logic spans 4+ places (_is_developer_email + _user_from_doc + register + login). Consolidate into the user-loading layer only, so downstream code never has to OR is_admin/is_developer.

_get_admin_config inserts defaults if missing — this is good. But on first call by a non-admin (which is correctly blocked at the route), the config is still created. Acceptable but worth a one-time seed at startup instead.
Admin config PUT strips empty smtp_global_password to avoid overwrite — good. Consider also rejecting clearly invalid emails for smtp_global_user via Pydantic validator.

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 executed; 1 skipped due to no seed documents — not a failure)

test_credentials: Admin: dragosserban95@gmail.com / Test12345 (developer + implicit admin). Fresh non-admin users registered via /api/auth/register with gdpr_consent=true.
seed_data_creation: Test created ephemeral users (test_user_*@example.com, test_403_*@example.com) — not cleaned up but namespaced for easy bulk deletion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: V5.4 admin endpoints validated end-to-end. Key gotchas: (1) GET /users/me is 405 — use /api/auth/me; (2) PV route is /fotovoltaic not /calcul-fotovoltaic; (3) regular_user fixture in tests is session-scoped — if you toggle is_admin via PATCH, USE try/finally to restore else later 403 tests will fail (use the fresh_non_admin fixture instead, which provisions a separate user). PDF download endpoint requires both an active project AND a valid photovoltaic_results object — the FE calc form correctly persists results to active project before enabling the PDF button.
rca_of_the_issue: No production bugs found. Only spec inconsistencies (route name + GET /users/me) flagged for documentation cleanup.
